skill.name: testing-patterns
skill.description: Practical Jest testing patterns for React and React Native projects, including factories, mocks, test structure, queries, and TDD workflow.
skill.summary: Write maintainable Jest tests with factories, mocks, render helpers, and TDD structure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and testing keywords as shell execution, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance. Manual review found TypeScript and Jest examples, plus normal npm test commands, with no malicious intent or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis matched Markdown backticks and TypeScript code fences as Ruby or shell execution. The referenced lines contain instructional Jest, React, and test utility examples, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown examples and inline formatting. No Ruby backtick operator or executable skill script is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged ordinary testing terms and sample identifiers as weak cryptography. The skill does not contain hashing, encryption, password storage, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual context confirms these lines discuss test descriptions, factory overrides, and describe blocks. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive transform appears.

low_findings.title: Benign Test Runner Commands Require Normal Caution
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for external command guidance, but low risk: the skill suggests standard npm test commands. These commands execute project test scripts and should be run only in trusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit npm test examples, so external execution guidance is real. They are standard development commands with no exfiltration, obfuscation, or destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged general testing and anti-pattern language as system or network reconnaissance. No commands collect host, network, credential, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are testing philosophy and section headings. There is no scanner, network request, environment access, or host enumeration behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams often write fragile tests with duplicated setup and unclear behavior coverage. This skill provides practical Jest patterns for factories, mocks, render helpers, and TDD workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of reusable test render helpers with required providers.
Shows factory patterns for component props and sample data.
Demonstrates Jest module mocks and GraphQL hook mocks.
Organizes tests with describe blocks, setup hooks, and behavior-focused names.
Provides query and interaction examples for React Native Testing Library.
Lists standard npm commands for running all tests, coverage, or one test file.
limitations: Examples focus on Jest, React, and React Native projects.
It does not include runnable helper files or package configuration.
It does not cover end-to-end testing frameworks in depth.
Project-specific providers and generated hooks must be adapted manually.

use_cases.title: Create Component Test Setup
use_cases.description: Build a reusable render helper and default props factory for a React Native component.

use_cases.title: Improve Test Maintainability
use_cases.description: Standardize test structure, naming, and mock cleanup across a team codebase.

use_cases.title: Practice TDD on a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Write a failing regression test, implement the smallest fix, and refactor after the test passes.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Basic Component Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a simple component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to write Jest tests for this component. Include a render helper if providers are required.

prompt_templates.title: Create Test Factories
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to create factory functions for this component props type and related test data.

prompt_templates.title: Mock Generated Data Hooks
prompt_templates.scenario: Component depends on generated query hooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to mock this generated GraphQL hook and test loading, empty, error, and success states.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Fragile Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to review this test suite for anti-patterns, duplicated data, mock leakage, and behavior gaps.

output_examples.input: A login form needs tests for input changes and submit behavior.
output_examples.output: A behavior-focused test plan with setup steps, user interaction coverage, and assertions against visible outcomes.

output_examples.input: A user card test repeats user objects in many test cases.
output_examples.output: A reusable user factory approach with sensible defaults and per-test overrides for roles, names, and missing data.

output_examples.input: A data component depends on a generated query hook.
output_examples.output: A mock strategy that covers loading, empty, error, and populated states without testing the mock itself.

best_practices: Write the failing test before changing production behavior.
Use factories for repeated props and data so tests stay consistent.
Assert user-visible behavior instead of private implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not assert only that mocks were called when visible behavior matters.
Do not duplicate large test objects across many cases.
Do not leave mocks uncleared between tests that share modules.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Jest with React and React Native Testing Library examples.

faq.question: Can it help with test data setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends factory functions with defaults and targeted overrides.

faq.question: Does it include end-to-end testing guidance?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on unit and component testing patterns.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents common npm test commands that users may choose to run.

faq.question: Is there any confirmed malicious behavior?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found instructional examples and normal test commands only.
